Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-17-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Nemecek, Esq. 415-894-5045 of Qian & Nemecek for Plaintiff.
Marco Quazzo, Esq. 415-956-1900 of Bartko Zankel Bunzel for Defendant PETER RADIN.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces the tentative ruling. Defendant requests to strike Opposition and Supplemental brief. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT PETER RADIN's Motion To Vacate Judgment.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Peter M. Radin, Jr.'s motion to vacate the default judgment entered against him on May 23, 2017 is denied. The Court finds, based on substantial evidence in the record, that Plaintiff Qian & Nemecek LLP dissolved on October 15, 2014, but remained in winding up status when it obtained the default judgment. (Nemecek Decl. 6, 7, 15 & Ex. B.) While in dissolution, the partnership could pursue its debt against Defendant, which is a routine and necessary part of winding up the business of a partnership. (California Practice Guide-Pass -Through Entities (TRG 2019) 4:290; Corps. Code 16954, 16802(a), 16803(c) ["A person winding up a partnership's business may preserve the partnership business or property as a going concern for a reasonable time, prosecute and defend actions and proceedings, whether civil, criminal, or administrative, . . . and perform other necessary acts." (emphasis added)].)

Plaintiff is entitled to prejudgment interest as a matter of right on the liquidated amounts Defendant owed it on the outstanding invoices at the statutory rate of ten percent (10%). (Civ. Code 3287(a), 3289; see State of California v. Continental Ins. Co. (2017) 15 Cal.App.4th 1017, 1038.) The amount awarded in the default judgment did not exceed that demanded in the complaint within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure Section 580. The complaint expressly alleged that Defendant was invoiced monthly and that payments were due within ten days after receipt of the bill, and the attached statement of account provided the dates on which the invoices were mailed. Those allegations were sufficient to notify Defendant that Plaintiff sought prejudgment interest on the amounts owed from the dates the sums became due and owing. (Watson Bowman Acme Corp. v. RGW Construction, Inc. (2016) 2 Cal.App.5th 279, 296.) The court properly awarded those amounts on the basis of the evidence presented at the prove-up hearing. (Nemecek Decl. (Mar. 21, 2017) 11 & Exs. B, C.)(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
